channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Gears E-Day and Halo Campaign Evolved Preorders To Go Live Today
item.content:encoded:
A reliable insider has confirmed that players can preorder Gears of War E-Day and Halo Campaign Evolved later today.

The XBOX Games Showcase and Gears of War E-Day Direct is all set to go live later tonight, and there are two games that players are sure to be a part of the event: Halo: Campaign Evolved and Gears E-Day. Now, players also know that tonight is the night when they receive release dates for both.

Gears E-Day and Halo Campaign Evolved Preorder and Price Details Revealed

The information regarding Gears E-Day and Halo Campaign Evolved preorders comes through a report by billbil-kun on Dealabs. The report states that preorders will begin right after tonight’s showcase for both titles. At the moment, there is some confusion about the PS5 version of Gears of War E-Day; however, the Xbox Series X/S and PC versions can be preordered later tonight for $69.99.

For Halo Campaign Evolved, on the other hand, a PS5 version is planned, but the report can’t confirm whether it will follow the same schedule as the other platforms. The price for Halo Campaign Evolved has been set at $49.99 USD and €59.99 EUR for all platforms. The release dates for both games weren’t revealed in the latest report; however, the release date for Halo Campaign Evolved surfaced a few months ago.

In other news, new information and images have begun to leak concerning Halo: Campaign Evolved’s preorder bonuses. Additionally, a new report covers details on the next mainline campaign project from Halo Studios and includes a job listing. What are your thoughts on Gears E-Day and Halo Campaign Evolved Preorders going live today? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the Little Nightmares 3 ‘The Backstage’ DLC. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Gears of War: E-Day PS5 Version Debate Sparks Online
item.content:encoded:
There’s some confusion around Gears of War: E-Day’s PS5 version, with one insider saying it’s not coming and the other pointing to evidence of a physical release on the platform.

We are just hours away from today’s event, the Xbox Games Showcase, which will be followed directly by a Gears of War: E-Day Direct. By then, everything will be clarified. However, just a few hours before this event, some conflicting reports surfaced online regarding the PS5 release of the upcoming title.

Gears of War E-Day PS5 Release Was Planned, but Now Canceled

The first claim comes from Jeff Grubb on the Giant Bomb podcast (via Wario64), who stated that a PS5 version of Gears of War: E-Day existed but is no longer on the cards. During the episode, Grubb stated, “Gears of War: E-Day was gonna be on PS5. It’s not anymore. That just happened. That was a decision that just got made.” Grubb is great with XBOX-related stuff, so players will definitely believe him when he says the PS5 release is not happening anymore.

Gears of War E-Day PS5 Physical Edition Evidence and Rating Found

Then comes in billbil-kun from Dealabs, and they have some conflicting findings. Their report states that “the SKU for the PS5 physical version of Gears of War: E-Day does indeed exist”. It has also been assigned a UPC; however, Billbil-kun has also added that they are unsure whether the identifier will be retained or removed. Additionally, Gears of War: E-Day has been rated by PEGI for PS5, and the rating has appeared today across all platforms.

It’s difficult to confirm whether the PS5 release for Gears of War: E-Day is still coming or whether XBOX is going the exclusive route. Could it be a result of the PlayStation CEO’s decision to no longer release single-player games on PC? We don’t know, but all the confusion about Gears of War: E-Day’s PS5 release should be cleared later tonight.

In other news, a shameless Gears of War and Dead Space clone had appeared on the Nintendo eShop last month. What are your thoughts on Gears of War: E-Day’s PS5 release? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the Avatar: The Last Airbender RPG no longer moving forward. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Cook And Sell Codes (June 2026)
item.content:encoded:
Use these Cook And Sell monthly redeem codes to get your hands on plenty of free rewards, including Cash, and other cosmetics.

Cook And Sell is a Roblox cooking and business simulation game is a fast-paced kitchen environment where speed, efficiency, and customer satisfaction determine success. Players must prepare meals, serve hungry customers, and strategically manage their growing food business while earning profits and unlocking new recipes and upgrades.

Success in Cook And Sell comes from mastering the complete business cycle: buying ingredients from your desk, unboxing them in the kitchen, combining them in your cooking pot, and displaying finished products to attract buyers. Players must scan customer purchases and process payments while maintaining inventory levels.

Currently in its alpha development phase, Cook And Sell has been building momentum since its launch but it has attracted quite a few players. So much so, that it already has over 19 million visits with over 180k favourites as of June 2026.

Active Cook And Sell Codes

  • BONUS – 200 cash
  • YUM – 150 cash

Expired Cook And Sell Codes

As of June 2026, there are no expired Cook and Sell codes

How To Redeem Cook And Sell Codes

Redeem codes in Cook And Sell is relatively simple, like the majority of Roblox games. Here’s how players can easily redeem any and all codes in Cook And Sell:

  1. Boot Cook And Sell on Roblox
  2. Click on the codes button on the left hand side of the screen
  3. Type or paste the code into the ‘enter code’ box
  4. Hit ‘claim’ to get your freebie!

Where To Get More Cook And Sell Codes

Cook And Sell codes don’t just materialize out of thin air—you need to know where the developers actually share them. Your primary hub should be the official Cook And Sell Discord server, where the development team drops fresh codes whenever there’s a content update, event launch, or player milestone celebration. These usually get announced in dedicated channels, so joining and enabling notifications is your smartest move.

Beyond Discord, follow the game’s official Roblox group page. Developers occasionally post codes there during special occasions or when the game hits follower benchmarks. It’s not as frequent as Discord, but it’s still worth checking. The creator’s Twitter or YouTube channel might also feature codes during promotional videos or community shout-outs, especially when they’re trying to boost engagement around a new feature.

Codes in Cook And Sell tend to drop around updates, seasonal events, or bug patch rollouts—but there’s zero in-game notification system. Thus, the easiest strategy is to save this guide in your browser and check back instead of trying to monitor every source yourself.

This page is refreshed as soon as fresh codes go live, and any codes that have expired are taken out quickly so you always see only the ones that still work.

Having fun using codes in Roblox games? Then check out the latest codes for King Legacy, Hypershot, and MonsterVerze. Also, check out the Insider Gaming Discord server.
